content: read reviews/950.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/950.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/950.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/950.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Copyright Header
     9→
    10→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12→
    13→## 2. Import Statement
    14→
    15→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    16→- [x] No other imports are present.
    17→
    18→## 3. Module Docstring
    19→
    20→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    21→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 950`.
    22→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/950`.
    23→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines f(n) as a sum over primes, asks about liminf, limsup, and little-o behavior).
    24→- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er77c]` with author and title.
    25→
    26→## 4. Namespace
    27→
    28→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos950 ... end Erdos950`.
    29→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos950`).
    30→- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements are present (none needed for this file).
    31→
    32→## 5. Custom Definitions
    33→
    34→- [x] `f` (line 38) has a LaTeX docstring describing f(n) = Σ_{p<n} 1/(n-p).
    35→- [x] `f` is marked `noncomputable` (involves real division and filtering over primes).
    36→- [x] No `sorry` in the definition of `f`.
    37→- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `f` (single letter, trivially conformant).
    38→- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    39→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).
    40→
    41→## 6. Theorem Statements
    42→
    43→- [x] All three theorems use the `theorem` keyword.
    44→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_950` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    45→- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib conventions: `erdos_950`, `erdos_950.variants.limsup`, `erdos_950.variants.little_o`.
    46→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
    47→- [x] All three theorems have docstrings describing the specific part of the problem they formalize.
    48→- [x] All docstrings reference the source paper `[Er77c]`.
    49→
    50→### `answer()` usage
    51→
    52→The original problem asks "Is it true that liminf f(n) = 1?", "Is it true that limsup f(n) = ∞?", and "Is it true that f(n) = o(log log n)?" — all yes/no questions ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies to all three theorems.
    53→
    54→**`erdos_950` (Part 1 — liminf):**
    55→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 50).
    56→- [x] The RHS formalizes "liminf f(n) = 1" via epsilon characterization: for every ε > 0, (a) f(n) > 1 - ε for all sufficiently large n, and (b) f(n) < 1 + ε for infinitely many n.
    57→- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
    58→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.
    59→
    60→**`erdos_950.variants.limsup` (Part 2 — limsup):**
    61→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 63).
    62→- [x] The RHS formalizes "limsup f(n) = ∞": for every M and every N₀, there exists n ≥ N₀ with f(n) > M.
    63→- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
    64→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    65→
    66→**`erdos_950.variants.little_o` (Part 3 — little-o):**
    67→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 72).
    68→- [x] The RHS formalizes "f(n) = o(log log n)": for every ε > 0, eventually f(n) < ε · log(log(n)).
    69→- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
    70→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    71→
    72→## 7. Category Attribute
    73→
    74→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
    75→- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
    76→- [x] All three theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies (consistent with `research open`).
    77→
    78→## 8. AMS Attribute
    79→
    80→- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
    81→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (11 — number theory).
    82→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single number, trivially satisfied).
    83→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    84→- [x] 11 is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    85→
    86→## 9. Proof Bodies
    87→
    88→- [x] All three open conjectures use `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
    89→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    90→- [x] No informal proof sketches for these open research problems.
    91→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    92→
    93→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    94→
    95→- [x] Variants are named `erdos_950.variants.limsup` and `erdos_950.variants.little_o` (correct naming convention).
    96→- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    97→- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
    98→- [x] Variants have docstrings describing which part of the problem they formalize.
    99→
   100→## 11. Code Quality
   101→
   102→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   103→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ε`.
   104→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   105→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   106→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
   107→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   108→
   109→## 12. Build Verification
   110→
   111→- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warnings only.
   112→- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.
   113→
   114→---
   115→
   116→## Summary
   117→
   118→**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)
   119→

The multi-part problem (liminf, limsup, little-o) is correctly decomposed into a main theorem and two variants, all with proper `answer(sorry) ↔ P` structure for yes/no questions. file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/950.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 950`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/950`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines f(n) as a sum over primes, asks about liminf, limsup, and little-o behavior).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er77c]` with author and title.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos950 ... end Erdos950`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos950`).
- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements are present (none needed for this file).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `f` (line 38) has a LaTeX docstring describing f(n) = Σ_{p<n} 1/(n-p).
- [x] `f` is marked `noncomputable` (involves real division and filtering over primes).
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition of `f`.
- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `f` (single letter, trivially conformant).
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All three theorems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_950` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib conventions: `erdos_950`, `erdos_950.variants.limsup`, `erdos_950.variants.little_o`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] All three theorems have docstrings describing the specific part of the problem they formalize.
- [x] All docstrings reference the source paper `[Er77c]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Is it true that liminf f(n) = 1?", "Is it true that limsup f(n) = ∞?", and "Is it true that f(n) = o(log log n)?" — all yes/no questions ("Is it true that ..."), so **Case 1** applies to all three theorems.

**`erdos_950` (Part 1 — liminf):**
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 50).
- [x] The RHS formalizes "liminf f(n) = 1" via epsilon characterization: for every ε > 0, (a) f(n) > 1 - ε for all sufficiently large n, and (b) f(n) < 1 + ε for infinitely many n.
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)` (inside the RHS of `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

**`erdos_950.variants.limsup` (Part 2 — limsup):**
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 63).
- [x] The RHS formalizes "limsup f(n) = ∞": for every M and every N₀, there exists n ≥ N₀ with f(n) > M.
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

**`erdos_950.variants.little_o` (Part 3 — little-o):**
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 72).
- [x] The RHS formalizes "f(n) = o(log log n)": for every ε > 0, eventually f(n) < ε · log(log(n)).
- [x] `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no" to the original question.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] All three theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (11 — number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 11 is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] All three open conjectures use `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for these open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants are named `erdos_950.variants.limsup` and `erdos_950.variants.little_o` (correct naming convention).
- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variants have docstrings describing which part of the problem they formalize.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ε`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warnings only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The multi-part problem (liminf, limsup, little-o) is correctly decomposed into a main theorem and two variants, all with proper `answer(sorry) ↔ P` structure for yes/no questions. The definition of `f` is appropriately marked `noncomputable` and has a clear docstring. No issues found.

1. **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

content.text: The review passed — **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
